titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/151

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=423 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:12:50.211159+00 | translation moa=424 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:12:51.703621+00

p.span:
. W . rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus terre sue . Clericis et laicis . francis . Anglicis . et Scottis . salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes .
me concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse ecclesie Sancte Marie de Mailros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem quam
Osulfus filius Vctredi et Vctredus filius eius contulerunt eidem Ecclesie de terra Ringwde . in omnibus ita libere et quiete . in elemosi
na perpetua  sicut liberius et quietius cartæ eorum eis testantur  et carta regis Malcolmi fratris mei concedit et affirmat . Teste
Ricardo Episcopo Sancti Andree . Engelramo episcopo Glasguesnsi . Iohanne abbate de kelchou . Osberto Abbate de Gedewrde . Nicholao Cancellario
Matheo Archidiacono . Ricardo . Capellano . Ricardo de Morauilla . Waltero filio Alani . Philippo de Valunnes . Valtero Corbet .
Roberto Auenel . Gilleberto filio Richerii . Bernardo filio breani . Roberto filio Guidonis . Willelmo de haia . Willelmo de Mor
temer . Radulfo namo . Imero clerico . Waltero de berchelai . Apud Lannarc .

div.p: William king of Scots to all worthy men of his land, clerk and lay, French, English, and Scots, greeting. Let those to come as well as those present know me to have allowed and by this my charter made firm to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God the donation that Osulf son of Uhtred and Uhtred his son conveyed to the same church in respect of the land of Ringwood, in all things so freely and quitely in everlasting alms as freely and quietly as their charters to them bear witness, and the charter of King Malcolm my brother has allowed and affirmed. As witness: Richard bishop of St Andrews; Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; John, abbot of Kelso; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Nicholas, chancellor; Matthew, archdeacon; Richard, chaplain; Richard de Morville; Walter son of Alan; Philip de Valognes; Walter Corbet; Robert Avenel; Gilbert son of Richer; Bernard son of Brian; Robert son of Guy; William de la Haye; William de Mortimer; Ralph le Naym; Imer, clerk; Walter of Berkeley. At Lanark.
